description_long: This oven features a compact, boxy design with a flat, square top surface that includes two circular stove plates of different sizes. The front face is equipped with a prominent, vertically-oriented door that has a barred window, likely for viewing the interior while in use. The oven is supported by four sturdy legs and is primarily constructed from a combination of metallic and possibly heat-resistant materials, with a dark brown and gray color scheme. A cylindrical pipe extends vertically from the rear of the top surface, curving backward, which likely serves as a chimney or exhaust for smoke and heat. The sides and back are plain, with the pipe attachment being the most notable feature. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, possibly wood- or coal-fired oven suitable for outdoor or garage use in urban environments.
description: A compact, box-shaped oven with a barred front door, two stove plates on top, and a rear exhaust pipe. The oven is finished in dark brown and gray tones, with a sturdy, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barred oven door, two stove plates on top, and the vertical exhaust pipe.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the plain side panel and the rearward-curving exhaust pipe.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel and the central exhaust pipe attachment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the side panel and the curved exhaust pipe.
attribute_control_panel_design: No dedicated control panel is visible; the top surface features two circular stove plates, but there are no visible knobs, buttons, or digital displays.
attribute_door_handle_type: The door handle is a simple, horizontal bar integrated into the barred door design, made of the same metallic material as the door, with a utilitarian, easy-to-grip form.
attribute_ventilation_features: A vertical cylindrical exhaust pipe is attached to the rear top surface, serving as the primary ventilation feature.
